MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Sum of the cohort's normalized, weighted process measures (patient experience, nurse staffing and communication, and cohort-specific process measures such as public transparency). Cohort: diabetes. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_NAME: SUMMATION OF STRUCTURAL COMPONENTS (U.S. NEWS VALUES)
USNWR_METRIC: Summation of Structural Components (U.S. News Values)
MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Sum of the cohort's normalized, weighted structural measures (patient volume and cohort-specific designations such as recognized cancer center or advanced heart program). Cohort: diabetes. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_NAME: U.S. NEWS COMPOSITE SCORE (U.S. NEWS VALUES)
USNWR_METRIC: U.S. News Composite Score (U.S. News Values)
MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Overall cohort composite: each quality measure min-max normalized to 0-1, multiplied by its assigned weight, and summed. Determines the overall rating band (top 30% / up to 500 hospitals = High Performing; bottom 20% = Evaluated). Cohort: diabetes. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_NAME: U.S. NEWS OVERALL RATING (U.S. NEWS VALUES)
USNWR_METRIC: U.S. News Overall Rating (U.S. News Values)
MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Overall performance band for the cohort: High Performing (top 30% of rated hospitals, up to ~500, subject to survival-based exclusion/inclusion criteria), As Expected, or Evaluated (bottom 20%). Cohort: diabetes. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Expected count of potentially preventable complications (Solventum AM-PPC v1.2) for the hospital's outpatient procedures, derived from national age-group x procedure-subgroup complication rates. Denominator of the outpatient complications O/E ratio. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Risk-adjusted unplanned inpatient readmission within 30 days of discharge from the index procedure, similar to the CMS hospital-wide unplanned readmission definition with cohort-specific criteria. Hierarchical logistic model on Medicare FFS patients age 65+. Categorical display band for 30-day unplanned readmission, assigned by statistical confidence of deviation from the mean: Excellent / Better than Average (>=90% / >=75% confidence better), Average, Worse than Average / Poor (>=75% / >=90% confidence worse). Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Risk-adjusted unplanned inpatient readmission within 30 days of discharge from the index procedure, similar to the CMS hospital-wide unplanned readmission definition with cohort-specific criteria. Hierarchical logistic model on Medicare FFS patients age 65+. Denominator: number of eligible index visits included in the 30-day unplanned readmission model for this cohort. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Risk-adjusted unplanned inpatient readmission within 30 days of discharge from the index procedure, similar to the CMS hospital-wide unplanned readmission definition with cohort-specific criteria. Hierarchical logistic model on Medicare FFS patients age 65+. Risk-adjusted odds ratio for 30-day unplanned readmission relative to the average hospital (derived from the hospital random intercept). Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Risk-adjusted unplanned inpatient readmission within 30 days of discharge from the index procedure, similar to the CMS hospital-wide unplanned readmission definition with cohort-specific criteria. Hierarchical logistic model on Medicare FFS patients age 65+. Model-predicted (expected) value of 30-day unplanned readmission for this hospital given its case mix. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Risk-adjusted unplanned inpatient readmission within 30 days of discharge from the index procedure, similar to the CMS hospital-wide unplanned readmission definition with cohort-specific criteria. Hierarchical logistic model on Medicare FFS patients age 65+. Hospital-specific random intercept (empirical Bayes estimate) from the 30-day unplanned readmission model; quantifies deviation from the grand mean. As published by USNWR, values are oriented so that greater than 0 indicates better-than-average performance. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_NAME: HOSPITAL READMISSION PREVENTION RANDOM EFFECTS STANDARD ERROR (OUTCOMES)
USNWR_METRIC: Hospital Readmission Prevention Random Effects Standard Error (Outcomes)

Hierarchical logistic model on Medicare FFS patients age 65+. Standard error of the hospital random intercept from the 30-day unplanned readmission model; reflects estimate precision (smaller with higher volume). Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Risk-adjusted unplanned inpatient readmission within 30 days of discharge from the index procedure, similar to the CMS hospital-wide unplanned readmission definition with cohort-specific criteria. Hierarchical logistic model on Medicare FFS patients age 65+. Min-max normalized (0-1) value of 30-day unplanned readmission used in the weighted composite score; 1 = best observed/theoretical performance in the cohort. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Risk-adjusted death within 30 days of the index procedure (displayed as 'Survival' on USNWR scorecards). Modeled with hierarchical logistic regression on Medicare patients age 65+, adjusting for age, sex, transfer status, admission year, 38 Elixhauser comorbidities, dual eligibility, and cohort-specific severity variables. The only P&C outcome combining fee-for-service and Medicare Advantage data (FFS/MA random effects weighted by payer mix). Categorical display band for 30-day mortality, assigned by statistical confidence of deviation from the mean: Excellent / Better than Average (>=90% / >=75% confidence better), Average, Worse than Average / Poor (>=75% / >=90% confidence worse). Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Cohort volume over the 5-year window (2019-2023). Higher-volume hospitals tend to achieve better outcomes; volume is winsorized at the greater of 200 cases or modified Z-score 3 to limit outlier effects. Minimum 15 cases required for rating. Data source: traditional Medicare fee-for-service claims. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Composite of two equally weighted subcomponents: the nurse staffing ratio (FTE registered nurses per adjusted patient day, AHA survey, capped at 2.4 per 1,000 adjusted patient-days) and the HCAHPS patient experience with nursing care linear score. Categorical display band for nurse staffing and communication, assigned by statistical confidence of deviation from the mean: Excellent / Better than Average (>=90% / >=75% confidence better), Average, Worse than Average / Poor (>=75% / >=90% confidence worse). Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Composite of two equally weighted subcomponents: the nurse staffing ratio (FTE registered nurses per adjusted patient day, AHA survey, capped at 2.4 per 1,000 adjusted patient-days) and the HCAHPS patient experience with nursing care linear score. Min-max normalized (0-1) value of nurse staffing and communication used in the weighted composite score; 1 = best observed/theoretical performance in the cohort. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

FTE non-supervisory registered nurses divided by adjusted average daily patient census (inpatient plus revenue-adjusted outpatient workload), from the AHA Annual Survey; ceiling threshold of 2.4 applied to reduce outlier effects. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Observed count of outpatient procedures followed within 30 days by a potentially preventable complication (ED visit or inpatient stay; Solventum AM-PPC v1.2) among Medicare FFS patients. Numerator of the outpatient complications O/E ratio. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Observed-to-expected ratio of potentially preventable complications following elective outpatient procedures (Solventum AM-PPC v1.2), winsorized at the 99th percentile. Values below 1.0 indicate fewer complications than expected. Note: USNWR flips the direction of this ratio for public display so that higher displayed values are better. Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Overall patient experience from the HCAHPS survey (overall hospital linear mean score, H_HSP_RATING_LINEAR_SCORE), 12-month rolling assessment of recently discharged inpatients. Group-mean adjusted for specialty vs. general hospitals in orthopedic cohorts; missing values imputed with the national mean (87.5 for 2025-2026). Categorical display band for patient experience, assigned by statistical confidence of deviation from the mean: Excellent / Better than Average (>=90% / >=75% confidence better), Average, Worse than Average / Poor (>=75% / >=90% confidence worse). Cohort: gynecological cancer surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Cohort volume over the 5-year window (2019-2023). MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Risk-adjusted death within 30 days of the index procedure (displayed as 'Survival' on USNWR scorecards). Modeled with hierarchical logistic regression on Medicare patients age 65+, adjusting for age, sex, transfer status, admission year, 38 Elixhauser comorbidities, dual eligibility, and cohort-specific severity variables. The only P&C outcome combining fee-for-service and Medicare Advantage data (FFS/MA random effects weighted by payer mix). Numerator/event count: observed number of 30-day mortality events among eligible visits. Data source: traditional Medicare fee-for-service and Medicare Advantage claims combined. Cohort: heart bypass surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_NAME: (HEART BYPASS SURGERY) NUMBER OF PATIENTS (FEE-FOR-SERVICE & MEDICARE ADVANTAGE DATA) (STRUCTURE)
USNWR_METRIC: (Heart Bypass Surgery) Number of Patients (Fee-For-Service & Medicare Advantage Data) (Structure)
MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Cohort volume over the 5-year window (2019-2023). Higher-volume hospitals tend to achieve better outcomes; volume is winsorized at the greater of 200 cases or modified Z-score 3 to limit outlier effects. Minimum 15 cases required for rating. Data source: traditional Medicare fee-for-service and Medicare Advantage claims combined. Cohort: heart bypass surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_NAME: (HEART BYPASS SURGERY) PATIENTS DISCHARGED HOME BAND (OUTCOMES)
USNWR_METRIC: (Heart Bypass Surgery) Patients Discharged Home Band (Outcomes)
MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Risk-adjusted discharge directly to home (self-care or home health) rather than to another facility, following the index procedure. SNF-origin admissions and hospice/AMA/IRF discharges are excluded; for medical and fracture cohorts the outcome is capped at the 75th percentile so SNF rehabilitation discharges do not preclude a top score. Hierarchical logistic model on Medicare FFS patients age 65+. Categorical display band for discharge to home, assigned by statistical confidence of deviation from the mean: Excellent / Better than Average (>=90% / >=75% confidence better), Average, Worse than Average / Poor (>=75% / >=90% confidence worse). Cohort: heart bypass surgery. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Modeled with a linear mixed-effects model on Medicare FFS patients age 65+; capped at the 75th percentile for medical and fracture cohorts. Numerator/event count: observed number of days at home within 30 days of discharge events among eligible visits. Cohort: hip fracture. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_NAME: PATIENTS RECOVER AT HOME RANDOM EFFECTS (OUTCOMES)

USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Whether the hospital is FACT-certified for bone marrow and tissue transplants (allogeneic, autologous, or immune effector cellular therapy). Cohort: leukemia, lymphoma & myeloma. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

Modeled with hierarchical logistic regression on Medicare patients age 65+, adjusting for age, sex, transfer status, admission year, 38 Elixhauser comorbidities, dual eligibility, and cohort-specific severity variables. The only P&C outcome combining fee-for-service and Medicare Advantage data (FFS/MA random effects weighted by payer mix). Standard error of the hospital random intercept from the 30-day mortality model; reflects estimate precision (smaller with higher volume). Data source: Medicare Advantage encounter (MedPAR information-only) claims. Cohort: pacemaker implantation. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Risk-adjusted death within 30 days of the index admission (displayed as 'Survival' on USNWR scorecards). Modeled with hierarchical logistic regression on Medicare patients age 65+, adjusting for age, sex, transfer status, admission year, 38 Elixhauser comorbidities, dual eligibility, and cohort-specific severity variables. The only P&C outcome combining fee-for-service and Medicare Advantage data (FFS/MA random effects weighted by payer mix). Numerator/event count: observed number of 30-day mortality events among eligible visits. Data source: traditional Medicare fee-for-service and Medicare Advantage claims combined. Cohort: pneumonia. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_NAME: MORTALITY RANDOM EFFECTS (FEE-FOR-SERVICE & MEDICARE ADVANTAGE DATA) (OUTCOMES)
USNWR_METRIC: Mortality Random Effects (Fee-For-Service & Medicare Advantage Data) (Outcomes)
MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Risk-adjusted death within 30 days of the index admission (displayed as 'Survival' on USNWR scorecards). Modeled with hierarchical logistic regression on Medicare patients age 65+, adjusting for age, sex, transfer status, admission year, 38 Elixhauser comorbidities, dual eligibility, and cohort-specific severity variables. The only P&C outcome combining fee-for-service and Medicare Advantage data (FFS/MA random effects weighted by payer mix). Hospital-specific random intercept (empirical Bayes estimate) from the 30-day mortality model; quantifies deviation from the grand mean. MEASURE_NAME: NUMBER OF PATIENTS (FEE-FOR-SERVICE & MEDICARE ADVANTAGE DATA) (STRUCTURE)
USNWR_METRIC: Number of Patients (Fee-For-Service & Medicare Advantage Data) (Structure)
MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Cohort volume over the 5-year window (2019-2023). Higher-volume hospitals tend to achieve better outcomes; volume is winsorized at the greater of 200 cases or modified Z-score 3 to limit outlier effects. Minimum 15 cases required for rating. Data source: traditional Medicare fee-for-service and Medicare Advantage claims combined. Cohort: pneumonia. USNWR 2025-2026 Best Hospitals: Procedures & Conditions.

MEASURE_NAME: PATIENTS RECOVER AT HOME RANDOM EFFECTS (OUTCOMES)
USNWR_METRIC: Patients Recover at Home Random Effects (Outcomes)
MEASURE_DESCRIPTION: Risk-adjusted days spent at home within 30 days of discharge from the index procedure; days in acute care, outpatient/ED/observation, or a skilled nursing facility count as days away from home.
